elements.sent-2411-021: A face of $P$ is the intersection of $P$ with a support plane.
elements.sent-2411-022: So the dimension of a face ranges from $0$ to $2$.
elements.sent-2411-023: The faces of dimension $2$ are called facets.
elements.sent-2411-024: The $0$-dimensional faces are vertices, and the $1$-dimensional faces are edges.
elements.sent-2411-025: Given a polytope $P$ and a face $F$, the active region $\mathcal{AR (F)$ is the set of all points $y\in Int\, P$ such that $F$ contains the base of some normal emanating from $y$.}
elements.sent-2411-026: The bifurcation set $\mathcal{B (P)$ is the union of the boundaries of active regions.}
elements.sent-2411-027: It is the PL counterpart of the focal set that appears in the smooth case.
elements.sent-2411-028: The bifurcation set is piecewise linear.

elements.sent-2411-032: The active region of an edge $E$ sharing two facets $F_1$ and $F_2$ is the intersection of the polytope $P$ with the wedge-like polyhedron bounded by two planes containing $E$ and orthogonal to $F_1$ and $F_2$ (these are blue sheets), and another two planes containing the endpoints of $E$ and orthogonal to $E$ (red sheets).
elements.sent-2411-033: The active region of a vertex $V$ is the inner normal cone of $P$ at the vertex $V$ (bounded by red sheets) intersected with the polytope $P$.

elements.sent-2411-039: If the base of a normal lies in the interior of an edge, the critical point is a saddle.
